notes: Do not build or run unsupported semantics as fake live scenarios.
Conditional scenarios require product support review before harness creation.
Every live verdict requires artifact validation, DB inspection, and log anomaly inspection.

scenarios.name: content-assessment-blocked
scenarios.reason: Assessment rollups model approved, request_changes, and rejected. blocked is currently a task-completion state, not a subject-decision outcome, and turning it into one would risk breaking existing assessment semantics.
scenarios.needed_support: Keep blocked as task-completion state by default. If a true blocked decision is ever needed, design it separately with explicit control-plane, verifier, and dashboard semantics.

scenarios.name: content-block-subject-policy
scenarios.reason: Blocking currently happens through existing rejected/request_changes resolution semantics. A distinct configurable outcome action path for block_subject is not clearly wired end-to-end through rule evaluation and continuity.
scenarios.needed_support: Implement explicit outcome-action handling for block_subject and prove it through subject/work-item/workflow state transitions.

scenarios.name: content-escalate-policy
scenarios.reason: Escalation exists as a task lifecycle behavior, but not as a clearly proven generic playbook outcome-action branch that can be configured and verified independently of agent choice.
scenarios.needed_support: Add explicit playbook-driven escalate action handling with deterministic continuity effects and verifier-visible evidence.

scenarios.name: content-retain-prior-assessment-on-rework
scenarios.reason: Current product behavior is revision-scoped invalidation. Assessments are evaluated against the current subject_revision, and there is no schema/config support to retain earlier approvals across rework.
scenarios.needed_support: Add revision-retention policy to the playbook model, continuity logic, and assessment aggregation semantics.

scenarios.name: content-terminate-branch-policy
scenarios.reason: terminate_branch is not an allowed assessment action in the current playbook model, so there is no legal way to author or execute this branch outcome.
scenarios.needed_support: Extend the playbook action schema and control plane with branch-termination semantics, legality checks, and operator/UI visibility.

scenarios.name: publishing-approval-before-assessment
scenarios.reason: The system can model approval before assessment only by splitting them across separate stages or checkpoints. Same-subject, same-checkpoint ordering inversion is intentionally not part of the current safe model.
scenarios.needed_support: Continue using separate-stage modeling unless a concrete need justifies a dedicated ordering design.

scenarios.name: requirements-human-review-blocked
scenarios.reason: Human review is currently modeled through stage-gate states such as awaiting_approval, approved, changes_requested, and rejected. A distinct blocked review decision is not part of the current safe model.
scenarios.needed_support: Model this as unresolved approval unless a real operator workflow requires a new gate outcome.

scenarios.name: requirements-stale-human-review-arrives-after-rework
scenarios.reason: Human review remains intentionally stage-gate driven in the near-term model, so stale approval invalidation is not yet expressed with explicit revision semantics.
scenarios.needed_support: Define stale-gate invalidation semantics at the stage-gate layer without promoting approval to a generic approval-task model in the current phase.
